title: Disable the Default Manager Credentials
objective: Secure the exposed management console by disabling its default credentials.
briefing: Logs show the manager console was accessed using admin:admin. That account needs to be disabled, not just have its password changed later.
hints: Restarting tomcat reloads the same insecure config - it doesn't fix it.
The fix lives in the manager's user configuration file.
debrief: The patch removes/disables the factory-default admin account in tomcat-users.xml entirely, rather than just changing its password (which could still be guessed or leaked again). For Security Misconfiguration (A05:2021), the durable fix is a hardened default: no standing default accounts, minimal attack surface for management interfaces, and a deployment checklist that verifies defaults were removed before anything goes live - not a one-time password change after the fact.

logs: [CONFIG] manager console accessed using default credentials admin:admin

patch /etc/tomcat/tomcat-users.xml --dis.output:
patched /etc/tomcat/tomcat-users.xml
default admin account disabled... done

logs_append: [FIX] default manager credentials disabled - console access now requires a real account
